## 🧪 Type of Change

<!-- Check all that apply -->
- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] 📚 Documentation update
- [ ] 🔒 Security improvement
- [ ] ⚡ Performance improvement
- [ ] 🧹 Code cleanup/refactoring
- [ ] 🔧 Configuration change

## 🔒 Security Checklist

<!-- Ensure all security requirements are met -->
- [ ] No sensitive information (tokens, passwords, keys) in code
- [ ] Input validation implemented for user inputs
- [ ] Rate limiting considered and implemented if needed
- [ ] Permission checks implemented where appropriate
- [ ] No SQL injection vulnerabilities
- [ ] No XSS vulnerabilities
- [ ] Security restrictions maintained and not bypassed
- [ ] Error messages don't expose sensitive information

## ✅ Testing Checklist

<!-- Confirm testing has been completed -->
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] New tests added for new functionality
- [ ] Manual testing completed
- [ ] Edge cases tested
- [ ] Error handling tested
- [ ] Performance impact assessed
- [ ] Security testing completed

## 📊 Code Quality Checklist

<!-- Ensure code quality standards are met -->
- [ ] Code follows TypeScript best practices
- [ ] ESLint rules pass without errors
- [ ] Code is properly commented
- [ ] Functions have appropriate type definitions
- [ ] No console.log statements in production code
- [ ] Error handling is implemented
- [ ] Code is readable and maintainable

## 🔍 Transparency Checklist

<!-- Ensure transparency standards are maintained -->
- [ ] Changes are documented in appropriate files
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md updated (if applicable)
- [ ] README.md updated (if applicable)
- [ ] New features are transparent and auditable
- [ ] Security restrictions are maintained
- [ ] Logging is implemented for new actions
- [ ] Changes align with transparency commitment

## ✅ Final Checklist

<!-- Final verification before requesting review -->
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes
- [ ] Any dependent changes have been merged and published
- [ ] I have followed the transparency and security guidelines
- [ ] This PR maintains or improves the bot's transparency standards

---

## 🔍 Transparency Statement

By submitting this pull request, I confirm that:

1. ✅ **Open Review**: This PR will be publicly reviewed as part of our transparency commitment
2. ✅ **Security First**: I have considered security implications and followed security guidelines
3. ✅ **Quality Assured**: I have tested these changes thoroughly
4. ✅ **Documentation Updated**: I have updated relevant documentation
5. ✅ **Community Benefit**: These changes benefit the Discord bot community

---

**🎯 Remember: Every change contributes to making Nexus Panel the most transparent Discord bot ever created.**
